embedding.qa_text: The letter is from the Legal Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission, dated January 19, 1990. It addresses a request for a legal opinion regarding the following: On January 18, 1990, I was advised by your office to request a legal opinion regarding the following: To whom it may concern: We are presently in the planning and implementation stages of a community development project, "The Wavecrest Redevelopment Project." The preliminary plan designating the original project area plus an extended survey area was approved 1/17/90 by the City Redevelopment Agency, on which I sit, for submission to appropriate taxing agencies for their appraisal and approval. Co-incident with this process, I was informed by City Staff and Legal Counsel, Mr. Robert Lanzone, that I might be disqualified as a sitting member of the Agency by reason of my conflict of interest in that my place of residence, 460 Polar Street, lies within the extended Arleta Park area of the extended area. I have an outstanding loan in the amount of $38,000 held by Foster Mortgage Corporation of Dallas, Texas holder of the deed. I would appreciate your legal opinion regarding my status on the agency; receipt of which, I would expect, as I was informed, within 21 days. I would appreciate any and all other legal ramifications of my status as it relates to this project and to the project itself. Any other legal referrals you might be able to provide would be most appreciated. This project was a major priority in my candidacy and is so far me as an elected councilman. It is an important priority for my constituency. I hope that a fair and logical resolution can be reached. Very truly yours, [signature] David Iverson Councilman City of Half Moon Bay CC Helen Bedesen, Mayor, City of Half Moon Bay Mark Weiss, City Manager Robert Lanzone, City Counsel Ted Tempert, Assemblyman, 20th District Rebecca Morgan, Senator, 11th District James Burns, Agency Manager The image appears to be a page of text in Russian, with some numbers and possibly a table or chart at the bottom. The text is mostly illegible due to the quality and angle of the scan, but there are a few recognizable words such as "1980," "1981," and "1982." The content seems to be related to statistics or data, possibly from a report or academic paper. If you have another scan or a higher quality scan, please provide it for better results.
